blurb: Here's a reason to head intown this Saturday. Porchfest turns an entire Atlanta neighborhood into a live music venue, with more than 100 local bands playing on actual front porches from 11am to 6pm. You walk the neighborhood, pop between performances, grab food from vendors, and spend an afternoon doing something that feels genuinely different from a normal concert experience. It's $10 to get in. For an afternoon of live music in one of Atlanta's most walkable, charming neighborhoods, that's an easy yes. Bring comfortable shoes and a good attitude.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness as a format, low ticket price, strong appeal for the educated adult demographic that appreciates arts and local culture. Virginia Highland is aspirational and familiar to East Cobb residents who spend time intown. Works for empty nesters and adults with older kids. Slightly lower audience match because it requires a drive and skews younger-feeling as a neighborhood, but the event itself has broad appeal.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for three days, and it never disappoints. Authentic Greek food, live music, traditional dancing, and a chance to tour the Byzantine sanctuary if you're curious. It's the kind of event that feels genuinely different from every other spring festival on the calendar right now. The food alone is worth the drive. Spanakopita, loukoumades, a cold glass of something good. This is a great pick for a weeknight date night or a low-key Saturday outing with the family. It runs Thursday through Saturday, so you have real flexibility. Don't sleep on this one.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the Perimeter audience. Greek festivals draw educated, curious adults who like food and cultural experiences. The three-day run with a Thursday opener is ideal for the professional crowd. Annual but not a weekly commodity, and it has authentic cultural depth that resonates with a higher-income, food-forward audience. The church tour element adds an interesting dimension beyond typical festival fare.

venue: The Vista, 2401 S. Stemmons Freeway, Lewisville
price: $10 adults / Free for kids 11 and under
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Lewisville Gun Show**
📅 Coming up at The Vista, Lewisville
🎟️ $10 adults, kids 11 and under free | [Learn More](ATTACH_URL_4)

If guns, collectibles, and Texas history are your thing, this is a weekend worth marking. The Lewisville Gun Show at The Vista brings together dealers, collectors, and enthusiasts for a full weekend of buying, selling, and browsing.

Veterans and active military get in free (first 50). Kids under 12 get in free as well. Admission is cash only at the gate.

This is a niche pick but a loyal one. If it is for you, you already know.
scoring_notes: Gun shows are a recurring format and not uniquely compelling, but this is a legitimate local event directly in the coverage area. North Texas demographics skew toward firearms interest. Lower uniqueness score reflects the recurring nature. Included as a niche-but-relevant pick for a segment of the audience. Not a top recommendation but qualifies.

blurb: Three days of seafood, live entertainment, artisan vendors, and outdoor fun at Thurman Springs Park. It's a full weekend event, so you can make it a Friday-after-work thing or a Saturday afternoon with the family. The food is the main draw, and for a seafood lover, it's worth building the afternoon around. Worth a look if you're planning something for the weekend and want to get outside.
scoring_notes: Decent fit but lower tier. Powder Springs is a bit of a reach for the Perimeter core. Seafood festivals have broad appeal but don't carry much can't-miss urgency. The three-day run with flexible timing is a positive. Better as a secondary recommendation than a featured event. Included because the food angle has real pull for a $105K median income audience.

event_name: Atlanta Braves Block Party at The Battery
blurb: The Battery is turning up the pregame energy this Saturday with a block party right outside Truist Park. Live entertainment, a festive atmosphere, and all the food and drinks The Battery does well, all before first pitch. This is the kind of setup that makes going to a Braves game feel like an event within an event. The Battery is basically in East Cobb's backyard, which makes this one of the easiest outings on the calendar this weekend. Grab your tickets, get there early, and let the Saturday take care of itself.
scoring_notes: The Battery is extremely close to East Cobb and the Braves are culturally central to this community. Block party format adds energy beyond a standard game. Uniqueness is moderate since it recurs on select dates, but the proximity and Braves connection make it a reliable, high-fit recommendation. Broad appeal for families and adults alike.

blurb: 125 artist booths, acoustic live music, and a family-friendly setup in downtown Duluth this weekend. It's free to browse, with food and drinks on site. The emphasis is on local and regional artists, so you're not looking at the same mass-market crafts you find at every other tent festival. If you've been meaning to find something for the walls or pick up a handmade gift, this is a good excuse to get out and look. Easy Saturday or Sunday afternoon.
scoring_notes: Decent fit for the Perimeter audience, particularly the family and young professional segments. Arts festivals with 125+ booths and quality artist representation appeal to the educated, higher-income crowd. Free admission with spend on art and food is a natural fit. Duluth is proximate to the Perimeter coverage area. Moderate uniqueness because arts festivals are common in spring, but the scale and local artist focus differentiate it.

venue: Old Town Lewisville, 201 W. Main St., Lewisville
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Lewisville ColorPalooza**
📅 Coming up in Lewisville | Old Town Lewisville
🎟️ Free (most activities) | [Learn More](ATTACH_URL_7)

Professional chalk artists. Sand art. Live music. Temporary art installations. Food vendors including CurryGirl, Smokd TX BBQ, and The Snowy Churro Texas. Strollers welcome. Dogs on leash welcome. Outside food and drinks allowed.

ColorPalooza is one of those days where you show up with the kids, lose track of time, and leave having done five things you didn't expect. It is put on by the Lakeside Arts Foundation and designed to feel like a full community day, not a quick stop.

Almost everything is FREE. Bring the whole crew and just go.
scoring_notes: Extremely strong fit for the family-heavy audience. Free admission removes income barriers for the mixed-income audience. Interactive arts format works for all ages. Stroller and pet-friendly signals broad family inclusivity. Local Lewisville event directly in the coverage area. Mommy Poppins coverage confirms family-first positioning.

blurb: Sherri Shepherd is bringing her "Make It Make Sense" tour to Buckhead Theatre on Saturday night. If you know her from The View or her hosting work, the stand-up is sharper and more unfiltered than you might expect. Relationships, pop culture, everyday life, all of it. Buckhead Theatre is an intimate room, which means you're close enough to actually feel the energy. It's a proper night out. Grab tickets and make a dinner plan to go with it.
scoring_notes: Solid fit for the adult-skewing portion of the Perimeter audience, particularly the 35–50 segment that grew up watching The View. Buckhead Theatre is familiar territory. Comedy shows tend to attract couples and groups looking for a structured night out. Lower uniqueness score because comedy at Buckhead is not rare, but the specific artist and tour name gives it enough specificity.

event_name: Pride: In the Name of U2 — Sounds of Lewisville
venue: Sounds of Lewisville Concert Series, Lewisville
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Pride: In the Name of U2 — Sounds of Lewisville**
📅 Tuesday, May 19 | Sounds of Lewisville Concert Series
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](ATTACH_URL_10)

Free outdoor concert. U2 tribute band. This is a very easy yes.

Pride: In the Name of U2 is one of the new acts joining this year's Sounds of Lewisville lineup, and U2 is exactly the kind of catalog that sounds better outdoors with a crowd around you. Bring a blanket, bring the kids, bring whoever you have been meaning to make plans with.

It is free, it is local, and it is a Tuesday night that actually feels like something. Go enjoy it.
scoring_notes: Free city concert series is a solid fit for this mixed-income audience. U2 tribute skews toward the 35-50 demographic which aligns with the median age of 36. Family-friendly and accessible. Not a one-night-only original act, but the free price point and local convenience make it a genuinely easy recommendation. Recurring series but each act is its own event.

blurb: If you know Marcus King, you already know. And if you don't, here's your entry point. The Grammy-nominated artist is reuniting with his full band for one night at the Fox Theatre, playing songs from his latest album alongside the fan favorites that built his reputation as one of the most naturally gifted performers in roots rock right now. He blends blues, soul, and Southern rock in a way that sounds nothing like a category and everything like a concert you'll talk about for a while. The Fox makes everything better, and Marcus King sounds like he was born to play a room that size. Get tickets before they disappear.
scoring_notes: East Cobb median age of 42 is squarely in the Marcus King wheelhouse. Blues-adjacent Southern rock with a mainstream Grammy profile hits the educated, established adult demographic well. The Fox Theatre is a premium experience that justifies a ticket price for this income level. One-night show adds urgency. Strong adult skew, which fits the mix of empty nesters and parents-with-older-kids in the audience.

event_name: Sherri Shepherd: Make It Make Sense Tour
blurb: Sherri Shepherd is bringing her stand-up tour to Buckhead Theatre, and if you've ever watched The View and thought she was the funniest one in the room, you already know this is going to be a great night out. The Emmy Award-winning host and comedian does stand-up the way she does everything else: unfiltered, warm, and with a lot to say about relationships, pop culture, and the general chaos of being a grown adult in the world right now. The Buckhead Theatre is a great room for comedy. Grab a friend, get the tickets, and make a night of it.
scoring_notes: Strong appeal for the 40-something female demographic in East Cobb. Sherri Shepherd has broad mainstream name recognition from The View. Adult skew fits the empty-nester and established-family audience. Comedy shows are strong date-night and girls-night draws. Buckhead is close to East Cobb.

event_name: Flower Mound Cultural Arts Festival feat. Brave Combo and The Western Flyers
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Flower Mound Cultural Arts Festival**
📅 Coming up in May 2026 | Flower Mound
🎟️ TBD | [Learn More](ATTACH_URL_11)

Flower Mound is pulling together a serious lineup for this one. Brave Combo headlining the pavilion stage alongside The Western Flyers and the Bombshell Dance Project, plus community performances from the Flower Mound Community Orchestra and the Lewisville Lake Symphony.

Brave Combo is not your typical community fest booking. They have been playing world music and roots for over 40 years and they are genuinely fun live. Combine that with a multi-stage local arts showcase and you have a full afternoon worth planning around.

This is a Flower Mound event but it is your neighborhood. Check the city's website for dates and tickets before spots fill.
scoring_notes: Flower Mound is a core affluent suburb in the newsletter's coverage area. Multi-act lineup with a legitimate headliner (Brave Combo has real national recognition) elevates this above a typical community fest. Lewisville Lake Symphony connection is a direct local tie-in. Strong family and adult crossover appeal.

blurb: Beer and cider tastings from more than 30 breweries, live music, and wildlife viewing after hours at Zoo Atlanta. This is a 21+ event, which means it's a very different experience from a regular zoo visit. You're wandering around the zoo at night with a drink in hand. The lineup of breweries is legit, the scavenger hunt is a fun add-on for competitive types, and the photo booth gives you something to take home. It's a genuinely fun date night. Grab tickets before the weekend.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the adult and young professional segment of the Perimeter audience. The 21+ zoo-after-dark format is genuinely unique and memorable. Strong date night appeal for the 30s demographic. 30+ breweries is a real draw for the craft beer crowd. The novelty of the setting (zoo at night) elevates it above a standard beer festival. Good price point for a $105K household.

blurb: Marcus King is reuniting with his full band for the first time in a while, and they're playing the Fox. That's a big deal. If you've only heard him on his solo records, the band version hits completely differently. More blues, more soul, more volume. The Fox is one of the best rooms in Atlanta for live music, and a Saturday night show there is hard to beat. Tickets are moving, and this is exactly the kind of show that sounds better the closer you sit. Grab them now.
scoring_notes: Near-perfect fit for the Perimeter audience. Median age of 38 with disposable income maps perfectly to a Grammy-nominated artist playing the Fox on a Saturday night. Marcus King's genre (Southern rock, blues, soul) has broad appeal across the young professional and empty nester segments. The Fox is aspirational but accessible. The band reunion angle gives this genuine uniqueness and urgency.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Brew & Que Festival**
📅 Coming up at The Lakefront, Little Elm
🎟️ TBD | [Get Tickets](ATTACH_URL_5)

This one is right in your backyard, and it has everything a Saturday on Lewisville Lake should have: live Texas music, serious BBQ, cold drinks, and a waterfront view that does half the work before the first band even plays.

Brew & Que is back at The Lakefront in Little Elm with five Texas bands this year. That is a full day of music, not a quick set between activities. Grab a plate, grab a beer, and let the afternoon stretch out the way it is supposed to.

This is the kind of local event that fills up fast because people around the lake know about it. Don't be the one who missed it.
scoring_notes: Annual festival but a marquee lakefront event directly on Lewisville Lake. Perfect for the lake-lifestyle community skew, family-friendly but also appeals to adults. Middle and upper-middle income families can afford whatever ticket price is set. Five live Texas bands plus BBQ is a strong value proposition. Timing during warm season is ideal.

blurb: If you've got a teenager in the house who lives and breathes gaming, this one's a no-brainer. DreamHack Atlanta is one of the biggest gaming and esports festivals in the world, and it's coming to the GWCC this weekend for three full days. We're talking Call of Duty League Major III, Intel Extreme Masters for Counter-Strike 2, open tournaments, cosplay, live content creators, an artist alley, and more screens than you can count. This is not a small LAN party. It's a legitimate major international event that happens to be 30 minutes from your house. A 3-day pass starts at $99, and family tickets let kids under 13 in free. If your kid has been asking about this one, the answer is yes.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews toward established families with teen and college-age kids, which is the exact DreamHack demographic. Parents may not attend themselves but will absolutely bring or send their kids. High uniqueness, annual but major international event happening locally. Ticket price is very reasonable for the income level. Strong parent-purchase angle.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for its 36th year, and if you've never been, this is the one to fix that. It's right in your backyard, it's been running for nearly four decades, and it draws a crowd every single time. Come for the spanakopita, the gyros, the baklava, and the loukoumades. Stay for the live music and traditional dancing that make it feel like an actual celebration, not just a food tent in a parking lot. Whether you bring the whole family or make it a date night, this is the kind of local tradition worth showing up for. Admission is free, so the only thing you're spending is on food. And you will spend it. Don't fight it.
scoring_notes: Hyper-local, right in East Cobb's backyard. Annual but with strong community identity and 36-year tradition. Free entry removes any barrier for an audience that appreciates value even at $118K income. Family and empty-nester friendly. Food and cultural events consistently over-index with educated, established suburban audiences. The proximity and local pride angle make this a near-perfect fit.

blurb: One of the largest FREE jazz festivals in the country, and it's happening in your backyard over Memorial Day weekend. Kamasi Washington and The Roots are headlining this year. That is not a typical free festival lineup. Piedmont Park fills up, the food is good, the vibe is easy, and you can come and go as you please across the whole weekend. Bring a blanket, bring some people, and plan to stay longer than you think. This one has been a Memorial Day staple in Atlanta for decades for good reason.
scoring_notes: Excellent fit across all Perimeter demographic segments. The headliners (Kamasi Washington, The Roots) are premium acts that would normally require a $60–100 ticket. Free entry removes all friction. The educated, professional audience that makes up the Perimeter core is exactly who attends this event. The Memorial Day weekend timing adds natural planning hooks. High uniqueness because the caliber of artists at a free outdoor festival is genuinely rare.

event_name: Endless Summer: A Beach Boys Tribute — Sounds of Lewisville
venue: Sounds of Lewisville Concert Series, Lewisville
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Endless Summer: A Beach Boys Tribute — Sounds of Lewisville**
📅 Tuesday, May 26 | Sounds of Lewisville Concert Series
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](ATTACH_URL_10)

There is no better soundtrack for late May in North Texas than the Beach Boys, and Endless Summer is bringing that whole catalog to Lewisville for free.

This is one of the new acts in this year's Sounds of Lewisville lineup. Warm evenings, good music, kids running around on the lawn, and zero dollars out of your pocket. That is a hard combination to beat.

Mark it now. This is the kind of Tuesday that turns into a great memory.
scoring_notes: Same series as U2 tribute. Beach Boys catalog is broadly appealing across ages and works especially well for the family-heavy audience. Late May timing is ideal for outdoor events in this region. Free price removes any barrier for the mixed-income audience.

blurb: Over 85 bands playing across more than 55 porches in Virginia-Highland, all in one afternoon. $15 gets you in. You just wander the neighborhood and follow the music. One block it's folk, the next it's a rock band crammed onto someone's front porch with a crowd spilling into the yard. There are food trucks, drink tents, and a finale concert in the park at the end of the day. It's one of those events you show up to for an hour and end up staying for five. Perfect for a spring Saturday. Get your tickets before the day-of price kicks in.
scoring_notes: Great fit for the younger professional segment of the Perimeter audience. Virginia-Highland is a natural draw for the educated, 30s crowd. The walkable, social format with food and drink options hits the right notes. At $15 it's a non-issue price point. The neighborhood exploration element makes it feel special and not like a typical festival setup. Good for couples and small groups.

event_name: Marietta History Center: American Presidency Event
blurb: The Marietta History Center has a new event centered on American presidential history, and if that sounds like your kind of Saturday afternoon, it probably is. The History Center consistently puts on programs that are smarter and more engaging than you'd expect from a local museum, and this one sounds like it's in that same vein. Details are on their website. Worth a look if history and local institutions are your thing, which for a lot of East Cobb neighbors, they are.
scoring_notes: Limited detail available from the candidate source, so this is ranked last and treated cautiously. The Marietta History Center is genuinely local and relevant to the East Cobb audience. Presidential history skews toward the educated, established demographic well. Lower scores due to uncertainty about event details, date confirmation, and uniqueness.

blurb: Powder Springs is just down the road, and this weekend it's hosting a three-day Seafood Festival at Thurman Springs Park. Think fresh seafood, artisan vendors, live entertainment, and a great excuse to get outside before the real Georgia heat kicks in. It's a full weekend affair, so come Friday night, come Saturday with the family, or make Sunday your slower, easier version of the same thing. This is a casual, local festival with a specific and craveable draw. Seafood and live music in a park setting is a hard combination to argue with.
scoring_notes: Close to East Cobb, family friendly, food-forward. Uniqueness is moderate since it's a recurring festival. But proximity and the seafood angle give it appeal for an affluent suburban crowd that enjoys casual outdoor food events. Ranked lower than Greek Festival due to less local identity and less confirmed detail on the event.
